mechanism-commit-innovation.description: Commit to one concrete mechanism-level innovation and keep scaffolding secondary to the task-solving path.
guardrails: Name the exact mechanism being introduced and the primary execution path it changes.
Keep one main mechanism; move supporting implementation details to notes or protocols.
Use the smallest validation suite that can falsify the core mechanism.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

alternative-path-contrast.description: Introduce a structured alternative execution or recovery path so rare regimes and fallback behavior are explicitly contrasted and stress-tested.
guardrails: Limit the number of new paths; one alternative path should address one concrete failure regime.
Make routing between primary and alternative paths explicit.
Validate the regimes where the alternative path should dominate or recover failure.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

surgical-modularity.description: Perform a localized block-level intervention with replace/rewire and prove the gain with focused ablation.
guardrails: Touch exactly one weak block.
Keep interfaces explicit after rewiring.
Include a mandatory block-level ablation.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

multi-scale-coordinator.description: Add a coordinator that aligns decisions across scales, layers, or service tiers without making coordination overhead the main contribution.
guardrails: Specify how routing conflicts are resolved across scales or layers.
Quantify the coordination overhead in latency or compute terms.
Keep validation centered on whether coordination improves the core task path.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

hierarchical-decomposition.description: Replace a flat control or execution flow with a hierarchy that assigns responsibilities across explicit levels.
guardrails: Separate responsibilities cleanly across levels.
Avoid duplicating the same control logic in every layer.
Show that the hierarchy improves control, analysis, or scaling rather than adding ceremony.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

feedback-closed-loop.description: Turn an open-loop process into a monitored feedback loop that measures outcomes and adapts future actions online.
guardrails: Define the feedback signal, update cadence, and stability boundaries.
Keep the loop lightweight relative to the controlled path.
Validate the loop under drift, delay, or stale-feedback regimes.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

theory-transfer-injection.description: Inject a theory-backed mechanism or invariant from another domain and wire it into the core objective or control rule.
guardrails: Name the transferred principle and where it enters the core path.
Limit transfer to one main mechanism instead of a bundle of unrelated ideas.
Add the minimum stress coverage needed to detect negative transfer.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

speculative-execution-with-repair.description: Introduce speculative or optimistic execution plus explicit repair so the system can pursue fast paths without giving up recoverability.
guardrails: Define when speculation triggers and how mis-speculation is detected.
Bound repair cost and rollback scope.
Compare against a conservative baseline at matched resource budgets.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.

resource-aware-adaptive-path.description: Add resource-aware path selection so execution adapts to workload, budget, or service-level pressure.
guardrails: Define which resource signals are observed and how path decisions are made.
Avoid hard-coded thresholds unless they are justified by system constraints.
Report the trade-off surface across quality, latency, and cost.
execution_logic: Generate the solution by instantiating the `atomic_blueprint`.
